title: or_abandon: language-level feature for resource leaks
description: ferrum-lang-concurrency.md §1.2.1 provides 'scope s timeout(5s) or_abandon { ... }' as a first-class syntax. The spec says 'Use only when leaking a task is acceptable (e.g. best-effort telemetry).' A language that provides structured concurrency as its only concurrency model should not have first-class syntax for violating that model. or_abandon creates orphaned tasks that may still be running on the thread pool after the scope exits — exactly the resource leak class that structured concurrency was designed to prevent. Making this a keyword-level feature (not a library escape hatch) makes it too easy to reach for. Suggested fix: remove or_abandon from the scope keyword syntax; provide it as an unsafe block annotation or a library function with a clear unsafe requirement, forcing callers to acknowledge the leak explicitly.
